title: DNS Switcher
tooltip_hints: click: panel · right: reset to ISP · middle: copy
tooltip_switching: Applying the DNS change — a password prompt may appear
err_apply_failed: Failed to apply the DNS settings
err_timeout: Timed out applying the DNS change (authorization prompt left unanswered?)
settings.providers.label: Built-in providers
settings.providers.description: Comma-separated ids included in the click cycle: google, cloudflare, opendns, adguard, quad9. Empty = none (custom servers and ISP default only).
settings.custom_servers.label: Custom servers
settings.custom_servers.description: 'Name = IP [IP2]' entries separated by ';' or ',', e.g. 'Pi-hole = 192.168.1.5; NextDNS = 45.90.28.0 45.90.30.0'.
settings.show_label.label: Show provider name
settings.show_label.description: Show the provider name next to the glyph (off = glyph only).
settings.poll_seconds.label: Poll interval (seconds)
settings.poll_seconds.description: How often the active DNS is re-read with nmcli.
settings.privilege_command.label: Privilege command
settings.privilege_command.description: Prefix to run nmcli changes as root (e.g. 'pkexec', 'sudo -n'). Empty (default) runs nmcli directly — NetworkManager's polkit policy allows this for active local sessions on most desktop distros.
err_not_authorized: NetworkManager refused the change (not authorized). Set the Privilege command setting to 'pkexec' or 'sudo -n'.
